pi_items.source: NinjaTrader 8 documentation and support forums confirm SPY is a real stock/ETF that can be added to instrument lists and traded in NinjaTrader 8

Official NinjaTrader 8 documentation at https://ninjatrader.com/support/helpguides/nt8/levelii_usingtheleveliiwindow.htm confirms Level II is a real window type opened via Control Center > New > Level II

Official NinjaTrader 8 Level II Properties documentation (https://ninjatrader.com/support/helpguides/nt8/levelii_properties.htm) lists all available properties and does NOT include any 'ShowOrderEntry' property. Level II Properties include: Grid font, Number of price levels (detailed/summary), Show details, Show quotes, Show summary, Size divided by 100, Tab name, various color properties (Price level X, Tracked market makers background/foreground, Up/Down tick background), Always on top, Show Tabs. The Level II window is exclusively for market depth data display; order entry functionality is provided by separate windows (SuperDOM, Chart Trader, Basic Entry) as confirmed by official documentation.

Multiple NinjaTrader 8 support forum discussions confirm the default workspace path is C:\Users\<username>\Documents\NinjaTrader 8\workspaces. The path in metadata is correct for the Docker user account.

privileged_info_summary: SPY is a real stock/ETF available in NinjaTrader 8. Level II is a real window type in NinjaTrader 8 opened via Control Center > New > Level II. The default workspace path for NinjaTrader 8 is C:\Users\<username>\Documents\NinjaTrader 8\workspaces. However, the required property 'ShowOrderEntry' does not exist in NinjaTrader 8 Level II windows according to official documentation. Level II windows are for market depth display only; order entry functionality is provided by separate dedicated windows (SuperDOM, Chart Trader, Basic Entry).
